message:
feat(core,abi): port simple wildcard matcher to core::string::wildcard (bd-wm-epic)

wordexp pattern expansion uses a simpler wildcard matcher (only `*`
and `?`) than full fnmatch — `[` and `\\` are treated literally. The
26-line glob_match_bytes in unistd_abi.rs is a classic two-pointer
linear matcher with single backtrack on `*`. Lifted to
core::string::wildcard alongside the existing fnmatch_match (which
supports brackets + escapes).

New core surface:
  pub fn wildcard_match(pattern: &[u8], text: &[u8]) -> bool

Semantics intentionally distinct from fnmatch_match:
  - `*` matches any sequence of bytes (zero or more)
  - `?` matches exactly one byte
  - all other bytes (including `[`, `\\`, `]`, `{`, `}`) are literal

Both matchers now coexist; callers pick based on whether they need
bracket character classes or backslash escapes.

abi changes:
  - Drop inline glob_match_bytes (~26 lines).
  - `use ... wildcard_match as glob_match_bytes;` keeps the single
    call site (wordexp_glob_match) byte-identical.

Verified via rch (CARGO_TARGET_DIR=/tmp/rch_target_frankenlibc_cc_bd-wm-2):
  - 17 new core::string::wildcard tests pass (incl. binary-safe NUL+
    high-bytes, multi-* backtracking stress, brackets-treated-literally
    distinguishing from fnmatch_match)
  - 173/173 abi lib tests pass
  - 3/3 wordexp integration tests pass
  - cargo clippy -p core -p abi -D warnings clean
  - cargo fmt --check clean

Closes bd-wm-epic.
